LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF STEFANIE NAIFEH'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION (PER EX PARTE ORDER OF 10/14/09). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR ORDER RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED, NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS OF HER CLAIM. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE OR AUTHORITY THAT OVERCOMES SECTION 2.5 OF THE PURCHASE AGREEMENT WHICH LIMITS JP MORGAN'S LIABILITY FOR BORROWER CLAIMS MADE ON A LOAN EXECUTED BY WASHINGTON MUTUAL. CC SECTION 2923.6 IS A STATEMENT OF LEGISLATIVE INTENT, AND NO "VIOLATION" OF CC SECTION 2923.5 IS SHOWN. NO EVIDENCE SUPPORTS A FINDING THAT DEFENDANTS ARE DEBT COLLECTORS UNDER THE RFDCPA, OR THAT A CLAIM UNDER THAT ACT WOULD APPLY TO THIS FORECLOSURE SITUATION. NO EVIDENCE SUPPORTS ANY ACTIONABLE FRAUD OR MISREPRESENTATIONS BY THESE DEFENDANTS, AND NO SPECIFIC INTENT TO DEFRAUD OR INJURE PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATED. NO CLAIM STATED FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. NO EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT THESE DEFENDANTS ENGAGED IN UNLAWFUL BUSINESS PRACTICES UNDER THE UCL. (MR. TANNATT TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., AND CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar, Dismissal of entire action filed on March 24, 2010. (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado; Court Reporter: Nichole Rodich, CSR #11604

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2010 Continued To Apr-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Plaintiff Stefanie Naifeh'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 10/14/09)
Off Calendar Mar-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Stefanie Naifeh's Order to Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order of 10/14/09). Appearances: Stefanie Naifeh, Plaintiff in Pro Per (323) 304-4033 Appearing thru CourtCall and moving party; Michael B. Tannatt, Esq. of Adorno, Yoss, Alvarado & Smith (213) 229-2400 appearing for Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Order re: preliminary injunction is denied, no reasonable probability that plaintiff will prevail on the merits of her claim. Plaintiff presents no evidence or authority that overcomes section 2.5 of the Purchase Agreement which limits JP Morgan's liability for borrower claims made on a loan executed by Washington Mutual. CC section 2923.6 is a statement of legislative intent, and no "violation" of CC section 2923.5 is shown. No evidence supports a finding that defendants are debt collectors under the RFDCPA, or that a claim under that act would apply to this foreclosure situation. No evidence supports any actionable fraud or misrepresentations by these defendants, and no specific intent to defraud or injure plaintiff demonstrated. No claim stated for declaratory relief. No evidence presented that these defendants engaged in unlawful business practices under the UCL. (Mr. Tannatt to prepare a form of Order for the Court's signature.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
